title: auth/DoA/doa-agent-access.spec.ts:34 › Delegated Organisation - Agent Visibility › Carol Clark (agent) submits for Plant Org 1 → Carol Clark (agent) can see it on her dashboard
summary: Delegated-authority (DoA) CHED-PP trace. A Plant-agency agent (Carol Clark, member of 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C') creates a CHED-PP on behalf of a DIFFERENT organisation ('IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1') via a two-step organisation-selection surface the main run never exercised, submits it, then confirms she can view/copy/amend her own delegated submission on her dashboard. The spec's sibling tests define the full who-can-see-what authorization rule set.
slugMappingNotes: consignment-organisation-type and consignment-organisation are the two DoA-specific organisation-selection sub-pages (see newSurface). The remainder map to the standard CHED-PP creation slugs already covered by the main run; they are listed for completeness but hold no delegated-agent-specific surface in this trace.
newSurface:
This trace exercises the delegated-agent organisation-selection surface that the standard (non-agent) creation journey never renders. It is a TWO-STEP flow, both under the /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/consignment-organisation?type=CHEDPP route, that appears immediately after the 'What are you importing?' (import type = Plants) page and before Country of origin:

STEP 1 — org-type page. H1 'Who are you creating this notification for?' (caption 'About the consignment'). Radio group with exactly two options: (a) the agent's OWN organisation, rendered by its literal name 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C'; (b) 'A different organisation'. 'Save and continue' button. In this trace the agent selected 'A different organisation'.

STEP 2 — org-list / delegated-org picker. H1 'Which company is this notification for' (caption 'About the consignment'; page <title> 'Which company is this notification for'). Radio list of the organisations the agent is authorised to act for, each radio label = org name + full address rendered as strong name over address lines. Options observed:
  - 'IPAFFS Plant Agency F' — 22 Acacia Avenue, London, N17 8LR
  - 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' — KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT  [checked/selected in this trace]
  - 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2' — KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6 UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT
  'Save and continue' button. Note step 2 renders only when 'A different organisation' was chosen in step 1; selecting the own-org radio skips it.

AGENT-ONLY HEADER NAV. Throughout the authenticated agent journey the account header renders an agent-specific link set: 'Carol Clark | Address book | Manage account | Manage trade partners | Sign out'. The 'Manage trade partners' link (/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/trade-partners/filter) is delegated-agent-specific surface not present for ordinary org members.

NOT observed in THIS trace (still gaps for a human): the Trade Partner badge on a consignment, and contact/importer auto-population from the selected delegated organisation. In this journey the agent instead created a brand-new consignor ('Linus George Ltd') by hand and set consignee via the 'Same as consignee' button, so no auto-population fired. These surfaces are named in the mining brief but were not rendered here; capture them from a DoA creation trace that adds an existing trade partner.

consignment-organisation-type.heading: Who are you creating this notification for?
consignment-organisation-type.caption: About the consignment

options.label: IPAFFS Plant Agency C
options.meaning: the agent's own organisation (own-org path — skips step 2)

options.label: A different organisation
options.meaning: delegated path — reveals the org picker (step 2)

consignment-organisation.heading: Which company is this notification for
consignment-organisation.pageTitle: Which company is this notification for
consignment-organisation.caption: About the consignment
consignment-organisation.control: radio list of authorised organisations (name + full address per option)

authorizationRules: An agent (Carol Clark, member of 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C') may create a CHED-PP on behalf of a DIFFERENT organisation she is authorised to act for; the delegated organisation is chosen via the two-step organisation-selection surface (own-org vs 'A different organisation', then a picker of authorised orgs). [confirmed — trace b1f31ed7 actions 10-13]

Agent submits FOR the delegated org (Plant Org 1) → the AGENT herself can view, copy-as-new and amend that notification on her own dashboard. [confirmed — THIS trace, actions 89-93: search CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525735 then soft-assert view-details, copy-as-new, amend-details all visible]

Agent submits FOR the delegated org (Plant Org 1) → a MEMBER of Plant Org 1 (Isabel Irwin) can view, copy-as-new and amend the notification on their dashboard — i.e. a delegated submission surfaces to the beneficiary organisation's own members. [inferred — sibling test doa-agent-access.spec.ts:7-31, not in this trace]

Agent submits for her OWN organisation (Plant Agency C, useOwnOrganisation) → a member of Plant Org 1 (Isabel Irwin) CANNOT see it (view-details hidden). Own-org submissions are scoped to the agent's own org and are NOT visible to unrelated delegated orgs — cross-org isolation. [inferred — sibling test doa-agent-access.spec.ts:60-86, not in this trace]

The agent account header exposes a delegated-agent-only 'Manage trade partners' link (/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/trade-partners/filter) alongside Address book / Manage account, indicating an agent maintains a managed set of trade-partner organisations. [confirmed — trace b1f31ed7 action 11/13 before-snapshots, header nav]

confirmedReference.evidence: trace b1f31ed7 actions 82/86 (#reference-number) and dashboard search actions 89-93

legacyCorroboration.orgSelectionPageObject: ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/OrganisationSelectionPage.ts:6-41 — radioDifferentOrganisation = getByRole('radio',{name:'A different organisation'}); radioOwnOrganisation = getByRole('radio',{name:'IPAFFS Plant Agency C'}); selectDifferentOrganisation() checks 'A different organisation', Save and continue, then checks the named company radio, Save and continue (two-step flow). Save-and-review variant also exists (btnSaveAndReview) for amend flows.
legacyCorroboration.workflowBranch: ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:259-264 — if organisationName -> selectDifferentOrganisation(name); else if useOwnOrganisation -> selectOwnOrganisation(). Org-selection step sits between Import Type and Country of origin.

notes: Trace is the second of three sibling tests in doa-agent-access.spec.ts. This one covers agent-sees-own-delegated-submission. The other two (org-member-sees-delegated-submission; org-member-cannot-see-agent-own-org-submission) were not in this trace but their authorization rules are recorded above from the spec source. Trade Partner badge and contact/importer auto-population named in the brief were NOT rendered in this journey (agent hand-entered a new consignor and used 'Same as consignee'); flag as open gaps to capture from a trade-partner-add DoA trace.
